accurate.why: All claims match the bill text and verified context; Section 546 of the PHS Act (42 U.S.C. 290ee-1) is the first responder training grant program and FDA is correctly the agency for drug approval/clearance.

no_hallucinations.why: Added context (FDA, overdose-reversal drug program) is accurate and the 'opioid-to-overdose' expansion claim correctly reflects the striking of 'opioid' throughout.

states_purpose.why: Clearly identifies the purpose as reauthorizing and modifying the first responder training grant program under Section 546 of the PHS Act.

changes_to_existing_law.why: Correctly names the amended statute (Section 546 of the Public Health Service Act) and describes the substantive changes to drug-coverage language, scope, and funding.

exceptions_conditions.why: The bill is a set of amendments without material new exceptions or eligibility carve-outs requiring mention.

effective_dates.why: Correctly notes the funding authorization period of fiscal years 2026 through 2030 and the introduction/referral dates.

major_provisions_covered.why: Captures all essential operative effects: broadened drug language, expansion beyond opioids, funding reauthorization, and the capitalization change.

objective_tone.why: The summary is neutral and descriptive with no advocacy or editorializing.

coherent.why: Well-organized and internally consistent, presenting changes in a logical, numbered structure.

concise.why: Compact with no padding or repetition, appropriately matching the bill's scope.

correct_entities.why: Correctly identifies the Public Health Service Act, Section 546, the FDA, the House, and the Committee on Energy and Commerce.

correct_figures.why: Accurately states the prior $36,000,000 (FY2019-2023) and new $57,000,000 (FY2026-2030) figures and the June 23, 2025 introduction date.
